author: @marcfruchtman9473
text: That reminds me... why do most steam locomotives show heavy plumes of steam coming out if the invention by James Watt allowed for re-circulation?

author: @jameshughes3014
text: That's a really cool history fact. I feel like sometimes, limitations are the key to creativity. When I sit down with a pencil and paper to draw a picture.. there's an infinite list of possibilities that are a bit paralyzing. But if there are restrictions or rules.. like, say, i'm plan to design some kind of dancing chicken.. suddenly ideas come quickly. I'm no fan of patents but it's cool to see that there are some useful things that exist as a result of them.

author: @binmanblog
text: You can't fault 3D printing for developing ideas

author: @jamesross1003
text: A work in progress  to me has a great deal more value than a complete and finished product.  Then again in the larger scheme of things I consider just about everything a work in progress.  It's all perspective I suppose.  Thanks Rob for all that you do!  I made a clock from a pressed cardboard in the past and have ever since been fascinated with gears and also steampunk things in general.  Add to that an obsession with nature inspired designs and a background in electronics and programming and finding just a single project to finish becomes well let's say a challenge.  LOL!  Trade of all jacks rather than a jack of all trades.  Thanks again Rob, I find you to be a wealth of inspiration and information.  The more I learn, the more I realize I have left to learn.  Keep doing what you are doing!

author: @Flashman36175
text: I really enjoyed the video,thank you.  Never knew much about Watt's invevtions other then basic steam engine, seems to be an interesting man.

author: @sinenomine9093
text: Somehow I suspect Watt's epicyclic drive is just a crank in disguise.

author: @somebody1869
text: Have you seen the Dakeyne Disc Engine? It was a steam engine using a nutating disc for power output. I would love to see your take on it.

author: @fieldsofomagh
text: James Watt is in good company. Nice bit of exploratory work into the history of these bygone machines.

author: @woodrowbrimm2805
text: Automotive Automatic Transmissions use a Planetary Gear Set also . The Simpson Compound Planetary Gear Set is used to obtain multiple forward gearings and a Reverse Gear. I'm a Retired Mechanic. Interesting Video.
